# Code of Conduct

Selftend project spaces should stay respectful and usable for contributors and users. This applies to GitHub, Discord, project email aliases, reviews, and any channel representing the project.

## Expected Behavior

- Be patient with first-time contributors.
- Critique work, not people.
- Credit code and non-code contributions.
- Respect differences in background, identity, language, and experience.
- Use care when discussing mental health.

## Unacceptable Behavior

- harassment, intimidation, or personal attacks
- discriminatory or demeaning comments
- sexualized language, imagery, or unwelcome advances
- doxxing or sharing private information without consent
- mockery of mental-health experiences, diagnoses, or coping strategies
- sustained disruption of issues, reviews, design work, or coordination
- using project spaces to promote products or services that conflict with the mission

## Mental-Health Context

Do not improvise crisis support in GitHub or contributor chat. If someone discloses urgent distress, respond briefly, point them to `https://selftend.org/crisis`, and move the technical thread back on topic or pause it.

## Reporting

Report conduct issues privately:

- `support@selftend.org`

Include what happened, where it happened, roughly when it happened, and any links or screenshots that help. Confidentiality requests will be respected unless they prevent action on the report.

Reports about the maintainer should use the same address. If that is not workable, open a GitHub issue so the contributor base can review the concern.

## Enforcement

The maintainer may:

- clarify expectations privately
- correct the thread publicly
- edit or remove comments, commits, or other contributions
- temporarily or permanently remove access to project spaces
- report abuse to GitHub, Discord, or other platform teams

Enforcement decisions will be explained, but they do not have to be debated publicly.

## Scope

This code applies inside project spaces and when someone represents Selftend publicly. It does not cover unrelated personal conduct unless that conduct targets project members or affects their safety in project spaces.

## Attribution
